title: Understanding Preferences: "Demand Types", and the Existence of Equilibrium with Indivisibilities
abstract: We provide a new taxonomy of valuations over indivisible goods using tropical geometry and the theory of tropical hypersurface arrangements, characterizing which classes of preferences guarantee competitive equilibrium existence.

title: The Tropical Geometry of Shortest Paths
abstract: We study min-plus (tropical) linear algebra and its connection to combinatorial optimization problems that arise in economic network models.

title: Product-Mix Auctions and Tropical Geometry
abstract: We use tropical geometry to analyze the product-mix auction of Klemperer, giving a geometric characterization of competitive equilibrium and its existence conditions via tropical hypersurfaces.
